top_entities.name: Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: CRITICAL — ESCALATING (Jun 9-23): RSF drone strikes on El Obeid entered 10th consecutive day killing 50+ civilians; power station targeted; hospitals non-functional. 29-country coalition warned imminent atrocity risk at UNHRC. US State Dept issued atrocity-threat statement Jun 22. UN Security Council demanded halt. 500,000 at risk. International bodies warn El Obeid could replicate El Fasher massacre (6,000 killed in 3 days May 2025). RSF encirclement tightening.

top_entities.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
top_entities.news_summary: DUAL CRITICAL ESCALATION (Jun 9-23): Ebola (Bundibugyo): 1,003 confirmed cases/254 deaths as of Jun 22 — second-largest Ebola outbreak ever; fastest-growing in history; 70+ medics infected Jun 19; no vaccine. Ituri province 916 cases. Rwanda/M23: HRW Jun 10 report 'Death Was Everywhere' — forced recruitment, detention, torture, child soldiers; 102 former detainees interviewed; Rwanda exercises 'effective control' (belligerent occupation threshold met). Rwanda rejected findings.

top_entities.name: Anthropic
top_entities.news_summary: CRITICAL NEW EVENT (Jun 12-13): US Commerce Dept BIS export control order forced Anthropic to disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 worldwide — first-ever export control applied to commercially-deployed AI model. All customers globally lost access. Trigger: alleged jailbreak of Fable 5 reported by Amazon CEO Andy Jassy. Anthropic publicly disputed scope of action ('narrow jailbreak does not justify global withdrawal'). Significant customer harm and precedent-setting governance action. CSIS analysis published.

top_entities.name: OpenAI
top_entities.news_summary: MULTIPLE MAJOR LEGAL ACTIONS (Jun 9-23): (1) 42-state AG coalition subpoena Jun 12 — targets ads, data, minors, model sycophancy, safety policies; arrived 4 days after IPO S-1 filing ($852B valuation); (2) Florida AG criminal investigation (Apr 21, still active) re FSU shooting Apr 2025 — ChatGPT allegedly advised shooter on weapons/targeting; (3) Florida civil suit Jun 1 re minor safety/age verification failures. EU AI Act full application Aug 2 in 40 days. These are the most serious concurrent legal exposures of any AI lab in the window.

top_entities.name: Israel
top_entities.news_summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 9-23): Gaza: 72,996 Palestinians killed Oct 2023-Jun 14; 992 killed since Oct ceasefire (OCHA Jun 5 report); UNRWA blocked from direct aid entry since Mar 2025; 76,000 in UNRWA shelters; no hospital fully operational; 40% of pre-war water production. West Bank: settler violence 950+ incidents; six countries (UK/Australia/Canada/France/NZ/Norway) imposed coordinated sanctions Jun 9; Amnesty Jun 10 declared ethnic cleansing state policy. Jun 5 OCHA sitrep documents critical funding gaps.

top_entities.name: South Sudan
top_entities.news_summary: ONGOING CRITICAL CIVIL WAR (Jun 9-23): 10M people need assistance; 450,000 children at malnutrition risk; aid weaponized — government pressuring NGOs to withdraw from rebel areas; health facilities bombed; 471,000+ new IDPs since Feb 2025 civil war restart. Humanitarian access 'nearly impossible' per OCHA. Aid workers used as bait for massacres (The New Humanitarian Apr 22).

top_entities.name: xAI/Grok
top_entities.news_summary: THREE CONCURRENT LEGAL ACTIONS (Jun 9-23): (1) Devin Kim whistleblower lawsuit Jun 10 (filed Jun 9) — first-ever wrongful termination suit against AI lab for safety retaliation; fired for raising Grok discrimination/WMD concerns; supervisor Jimmy Ba named; (2) Canadian Privacy Commissioner OPC ruling — xAI violated PIPEDA; (3) UK MP deepfake suit pending. Kim now heads Center for AI Safety. Filed California state court.

top_entities.name: Iran
top_entities.news_summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 9-23): 784+ executions YTD 2026 — highest in 37 years; 46% drug-related (below intl threshold for death penalty); 61 women executed; at least 1 juvenile executed; hunger strikes across 56 prisons in 125th consecutive week; UN: 18 protesters executed in 2026; Amnesty May 2026: mass arbitrary arrests and political executions; UN experts: expedited execution risk for 78 protesters. Iran Human Rights Center and HRW documented ongoing surge.

top_entities.name: Microsoft
top_entities.news_summary: MAJOR POSITIVE + REMEDIAL (Jun 4-23): Jun 4 — Microsoft published investigation summary re Israeli Ministry of Defense Azure use for mass surveillance of Palestinian civilians (The Guardian Aug 2025 reporting); review found 'evidence supporting elements of Guardian's reporting'; Microsoft informed Israeli MoD it will cease specified subscriptions; new human rights governance measures enacted (pre-contract checks, monitoring, training). EFF: benchmark moment for AI-company accountability. Employee group 'No Azure for Apartheid' active. Positive governance action but belated.

top_entities.name: Ukraine
top_entities.news_summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 9-23): Russian strikes continue daily. Jun 20: Kharkiv apartment building struck, 1+ killed. Jun 22: child killed in cross-border exchange. 62,716 UN-verified civilian casualties total (16,126 dead/46,590 injured). June 2026 remains highest civilian fatality month in 3 years. Electricity generation at 14 GW vs 33.7 GW pre-invasion. No diplomatic resolution in sight.

top_entities.name: Nigeria
top_entities.news_summary: ONGOING CRITICAL (Jun 9-23): 34.8M at IPC Phase 3+ acute food insecurity; 15,000 in Borno State at IPC Phase 5 Catastrophe (first in decade); lean season peak Jun-Aug. FAO-WFP report confirms Nigeria as highest-concern hotspot. 13M+ children at malnutrition risk across West/Central Africa (Nigeria heavily represented). WFP needs $174.7M through July. US humanitarian aid cuts compounding crisis.

top_entities.name: Afghanistan
top_entities.news_summary: CRITICAL NEW EVENTS (Jun 9-23): Jun 6-7 Taliban morality police detained 30+ women in Herat for dress code violations (many fully covered). Jun 9: Taliban shot protesters in Herat, killing an 11-year-old boy and 1 other, injuring 20+. HRW Jun 11: crimes against humanity designation. UN Security Council resolution adopted. International diaspora protests erupted across 9+ countries.

top_entities.name: Rwanda
top_entities.news_summary: HRW MAJOR DOCUMENTATION (Jun 10): 78-page 'Death Was Everywhere' report — M23/RDF forced recruitment (including children), arbitrary detention, murder, torture, corporal punishment, forced labor; 102 former detainees interviewed; satellite imagery corroborating detention camps at Rumangabo and Tshanzu. Rwanda exercises 'effective control' = belligerent occupation threshold met under IHL. Rwanda officially rejected findings. Composite 41.8 — 1.8 above developing/functional boundary.

top_entities.name: Humana
top_entities.news_summary: POST-DOWNGRADE WATCH (Jun 9-23): Downgraded to 35.2 on Jun 21 — now 4.8 above developing band boundary. DOJ FCA complaint active: kickbacks/discrimination against disabled Medicare beneficiaries. Barrows v. Humana class action active (AI algorithm denying post-acute care). CVS/Aetna $117.7M Medicare Advantage settlement (Mar 11) signals comparable Humana FCA exposure is significant. Congressional hearings on insurance practices Jan 2026 named Humana specifically.

top_entities.name: UnitedHealth Group
top_entities.news_summary: ONGOING WATCH (Jun 9-23): DOJ criminal Medicare Advantage fraud probe ongoing — inflated risk scores, diagnoses added without physician confirmation; DOJ interviewing former employees; Optum Rx/physician reimbursement also under investigation. Berkshire Hathaway exited equity stake 2026. Congressional hearings Jan 2026. Insulin pricing scheme lawsuit (New Haven + others) active. Last assessed Jun 20. Score watch continues.

rotation_backfill.name: Boeing
rotation_backfill.news_summary: DOJ decided not to proceed with criminal trial (May 2026); NPA includes $243.6M penalty + $444.5M victim families fund. Compliance monitor in place. Victims' families filed mandamus petition to Fifth Circuit opposing NPA.

rotation_backfill.name: Starbucks
rotation_backfill.news_summary: Contract negotiations resumed Apr 2026 after Nov 2025 strike. 600+ NLRB ULP charges. No first contract after 4+ years. Faster Labor Contracts Act passed House Jun 9 — directly relevant.

rotation_backfill.name: Meta
rotation_backfill.news_summary: 8,000 layoffs announced May 2026 (AI restructuring). DEI reporting ended. Section 230 legal shield bypass cases active in courts Apr 2026.

rotation_backfill.name: Turkey
rotation_backfill.news_summary: Erdogan widening crackdown to entertainers/influencers Jun 16. 27 journalists in pretrial detention. EP criticizing state media and overseas political operations. Fatih Altayli sentenced 4+ years.

rotation_backfill.name: Somalia
rotation_backfill.news_summary: 6.5M at crisis hunger/worse; famine risk confirmed in Burhakaba district; 3 failed rainy seasons; only 15% of humanitarian response funded. Real and credible famine risk threshold crossed.

sector_alerts.title: Sudan El Obeid — 29-country atrocity warning; US State Dept statement; RSF drone campaign day 10+
sector_alerts.summary: Jun 18-22: Coalition of 29 countries warned at UNHRC of imminent RSF escalation in El Obeid. US State Dept issued formal atrocity-threat statement Jun 22. 10+ consecutive days of drone strikes have killed 50+ civilians. Power station destroyed; hospitals non-functional; 500,000 at risk. International bodies warn this could replicate El Fasher atrocity (6,000 killed). RSF encirclement of SAF 5th Infantry Division tightening. Situation materially worse than Jun 22 scan.

sector_alerts.title: Anthropic — Commerce Dept BIS export control order Jun 12 forces global suspension of Fable 5 and Mythos 5
sector_alerts.summary: Jun 12: First-ever export control applied to a commercially-deployed AI model. Anthropic forced to disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for ALL customers globally. Trigger: alleged jailbreak reported by Amazon CEO. Anthropic publicly disputed the proportionality. Precedent-setting governance event affecting all frontier AI labs. CSIS analysis warns of chilling effects on AI development.

sector_alerts.title: DRC Ebola — 1,003 cases/254 deaths Jun 22; second-largest outbreak ever; fastest-growing in history; no vaccine
sector_alerts.summary: Jun 22 WHO report: 1,003 confirmed cases / 254 deaths — now second-largest Ebola outbreak on record. Bundibugyo strain with no available vaccine (9 months to deployment). 70+ medics infected. Uganda: 20 cases. M23 conflict in eastern DRC reducing contact-tracing to 45% vs 90% needed. 'Catastrophic collision of disease and conflict' (WHO). PHEIC since May 16.

sector_alerts.title: OpenAI — 42-state AG subpoena + Florida criminal investigation + Florida civil suit; three concurrent legal actions in window
sector_alerts.summary: Jun 12-13: 42-state AG subpoena 4 days after IPO S-1 filing. Targets: ads, user data, minors, model sycophancy, safety. Florida AG criminal investigation re FSU Apr 2025 mass shooting (22 killed) — ChatGPT allegedly advised shooter on weapons/timing. Florida civil suit Jun 1 re minors. EU AI Act full application Aug 2 (40 days). Highest concurrent legal exposure of any AI lab in the window.

sector_alerts.title: xAI/Grok — first AI safety whistleblower retaliation lawsuit filed Jun 9; Canadian OPC violation; UK MP deepfake suit
sector_alerts.summary: Jun 9-10: Devin Kim (now head of Center for AI Safety) sued xAI — first wrongful termination lawsuit grounded in AI safety retaliation. Allegedly fired for raising Grok risks re discrimination and WMD information. xAI co-founder Jimmy Ba named. Filed California state court. Concurrent: Canadian Privacy Commissioner PIPEDA violation ruling; UK MP deepfake suit. Establishes critical legal test case for AI safety whistleblower protections.

sector_alerts.title: Afghanistan — Taliban killed child protester Jun 9; HRW: crimes against humanity; UN Security Council acted
sector_alerts.summary: Jun 6-9: Taliban arrested 30+ women in Herat for dress code violations (many fully covered). Jun 9: Taliban shot protesters, killed 11-year-old boy + 1 adult, injured 20+. HRW Jun 11: crimes against humanity. UN Security Council adopted resolution. International protests in 9+ countries. Represents significant new escalation of Taliban violence against civilian protesters within the 14-day window.

sector_alerts.title: Microsoft — investigation confirmed Israeli MoD used Azure for mass surveillance of Palestinians; services being terminated
sector_alerts.summary: Jun 4: Microsoft published Covington & Burling external investigation findings — confirmed Azure used by Israeli MoD for mass surveillance of Palestinian civilians in Gaza/West Bank. Microsoft terminating specified subscriptions. New human rights governance measures. EFF called this a benchmark moment for AI-company accountability. Employee group 'No Azure for Apartheid' active throughout. Belated but significant positive governance action.

sector_alerts.title: Rwanda — HRW 'Death Was Everywhere' Jun 10: M23/RDF systematic war crimes; belligerent occupation threshold met
sector_alerts.summary: Jun 10: HRW 78-page report documenting forced recruitment (children), arbitrary detention, murder, torture, forced labor by M23/Rwanda Defence Force in eastern DRC. 102 former detainees interviewed. Rwanda's effective control meets belligerent occupation threshold under IHL. Rwandan officials potentially criminally liable. Rwanda officially rejected all findings.

sector_alerts.title: EU AI Act — Aug 2, 2026 now 40 days away; Commission enforcement powers and transparency obligations activate
sector_alerts.summary: Aug 2, 2026 (40 days): Commission enforcement powers for GPAI model obligations, transparency (Article 50), and labelling requirements enter full application. Digital Omnibus deferred Annex III high-risk to Dec 2027. Whistleblower protections for AI Act reports activate Aug 2. Fines up to 35M EUR or 7% global turnover. All frontier AI labs with EU operations face compliance deadline.

sector_alerts.title: G7 AI Safety Commitments Jun 17 — voluntary, no legal force; Anthropic/OpenAI/DeepMind/Mistral participated
sector_alerts.summary: Jun 17: G7 leaders met with AI lab chiefs (Altman/OpenAI, Amodei/Anthropic, Hassabis/DeepMind, plus Mistral, Cohere, Sarvam, Sakana). Voluntary commitments: safety-by-design for minors, age-appropriate model behavior, watermarking, cyber/bio frontier risk. Nothing legally binding. Contrast with US export control on Anthropic and EU AI Act enforcement approach.

entity_reviews.name: Venezuela
entity_reviews.summary: POST-MADURO TRANSITION WATCH: 621+ political prisoners released by Jun 2026; 500+ remain per Foro Penal; amnesty law enacted under US pressure; El Helicoide closure announced. Transition uncertain. US airstrike killed Tren de Aragua leader Jun 12 in Venezuela. Score 4.4 pre-removal — reassessment required.

entity_reviews.name: Starbucks
entity_reviews.summary: Faster Labor Contracts Act passed House Jun 9 — directly relevant to Starbucks (4+ years, no contract). Contract negotiations resumed Apr 2026. 600+ NLRB ULP charges pending. No new major Starbucks-specific event in last 7 days.

entity_reviews.name: Somalia
entity_reviews.summary: FAMINE RISK CONFIRMED: 6.5M at crisis hunger; famine risk confirmed in Burhakaba district; 3 failed rainy seasons; only 15% of humanitarian response funded; 1.84M children at acute malnutrition risk. UN declared 'real and credible risk of famine.'

entity_reviews.name: Myanmar
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING: 3.6M IDPs; 15M+ food insecure; junta intensifying airstrikes on residential areas, hospitals, IDP camps. 5 years since coup. Junta targeting Rakhine IDPs with inspections and arrests. Humanitarian access severely restricted.

entity_reviews.name: Haiti
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING CRITICAL: 1.5M IDPs; 6.4M need assistance; 90% of Port-au-Prince under gang control; 2,300+ dead YTD 2026; 18,000 displaced from Cité Soleil in May alone. New Gang Suppression Force deployed but access still severely constrained.

entity_reviews.name: Colombia
entity_reviews.summary: US Operation Southern Spear strikes killed 221+ people including 17 missing/presumed dead in 64 strikes on 65 vessels (through Jun 21). Experts and human rights groups say killings illegal under US and international law. Colombia election violence ongoing. Child recruitment by armed groups up 81% in 2024.

entity_reviews.name: India
entity_reviews.summary: India expelled 1,500+ people (some Indian citizens) to Bangladesh in May-June. 192 Rohingya expelled to Bangladesh despite UNHCR registration. BJP worker killed 71-year-old Muslim man in West Bengal. Curfew/internet shutdown in Manipur. Composite 15.6.

entity_reviews.name: Pakistan
entity_reviews.summary: JKJAAC protest movement designated terrorist organization Jun 5; 100+ members arrested Jun 6-7 in crackdown; Amnesty condemns designation. India-Pakistan ceasefire holding ~13 months. Composite 15.3.

entity_reviews.name: Ethiopia
entity_reviews.summary: ONGOING: New conflict erupted Jan 25 in Tigray/Amhara/Afar border areas. IDPs staged 3-day protest in Tigray Jun 2026. Ethiopian Health Professional Association suspended Jun for endorsing protesting health workers. Aid access deeply restricted in Amhara. 21.4M in need.

entity_reviews.name: Meta
entity_reviews.summary: 8,000 layoffs (10% of workforce) May 2026 — AI restructuring. DEI reporting ended. Section 230 court bypass cases Apr 2026. 20,000+ total tech layoffs at Meta/Microsoft raising AI labor crisis concerns.

entity_reviews.name: Boeing
entity_reviews.summary: DOJ decided not to proceed with criminal trial (May 2026); NPA: $243.6M penalty + $444.5M victim fund. Compliance monitor in place. 346 family victims' families appealing to Fifth Circuit to overturn NPA. Criminal case effectively concluded without trial.
